trade.summary: India exports engineering goods, pharmaceuticals, textiles, and IT services to Sweden. Sweden exports machinery, telecommunications equipment (Ericsson), vehicles (Volvo, Scania), and medical devices. Ericsson's significant R&D and operations in India makes it a major business relationship. Swedish companies are active in Indian manufacturing and telecom.

diplomatic.summary: India and Sweden signed a 'Joint Action Plan for India-Sweden Innovation Partnership' in 2015. Sweden is an important European partner for India in technology, innovation, and clean energy. Both countries cooperate through EU-India channels. Sweden has been an advocate for stronger EU-India ties. PM Modi visited Sweden in 2018.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation. Saab has sold Gripen fighters (though India ultimately chose Rafale in the MMRCA contest). India-Sweden defence cooperation includes electronics, radar systems, and submarines. A bilateral defence MoU has been signed. Saab continues to engage with India on future procurement.

financial.summary: Swedish FDI in India is significant, anchored by Ericsson, Volvo, ABB, H&M, IKEA, and Atlas Copco. Indian IT companies have major presence in Sweden serving European clients. IKEA's sourcing from India is substantial. Growing investment in Swedish-Indian startups.

energy.summary: Sweden and India cooperate on renewable energy and smart grid technology. ABB's energy management solutions are deployed in India. Sweden is part of the International Solar Alliance (ISA) co-founded by India. Cooperation on nuclear energy technology is under discussion.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation. Swedish-Indian Research and Innovation Partnership (SIRIC) programme. Joint research in life sciences, ICT, clean energy, and materials science. Many Indian researchers at Swedish institutions. Ericsson's R&D centres in India are a major cooperation node.
